en.text: thee. the upright: or, they love thee uprightly
not upon me, because I am black
me, O thou whom my soul
thou makest thy flock to rest
why should I be as one that turneth aside
? as one: or, as one that is veiled
is unto me as a cluster
. I sat: Heb. I delighted and sat down, etc taste: Heb. palate
. comfort: Heb. straw me with apples
. I charge: Heb. I adjure you
is mine, and I am his: he feedeth
me: to whom I said, Saw
him, and would not let him go
. that: or, that eat of, etc
of sheep that are even shorn
. inclosed: Heb. barred shut up: Heb. barred
. yea: or, and be drunken with loves
of the door, and my bowels
for him. for him: or, (as some read) in me
. sweet: Heb. passing, or, running about
him, but I could not find
him, but he gave me no answer
. the chiefest: Heb. a standard bearer
are as the eyes of doves
. fitly: Heb. sitting in fulness, that is, fitly placed, and set as a precious stone in the foil of a ring
. sweet flowers: or, towers of perfumes
, and there is not one barren
Who is she that looketh forth
. Or ever: Heb. I knew not made: or, set me on the chariots of my willing people
upon thee. What will ye see
. Carmel: or, crimson held: Heb. bound
And the roof of thy mouth
. sweetly: Heb. straightly of those: or, of the ancient
thee; yea, I should not be despised
. I should not: Heb. they should not despise me
me: I would cause thee to drink
. that: Heb. why should ye stir up, or, why, etc
Who is this that cometh up
thee up under the apple tree
, which hath a most vehement flame
when she shall be spoken for
: and if she be a door
: then was I in his eyes
